levels.label: First Sail
levels.description: Welcome aboard! Everyone starts here before their first Disney cruise.

benefits.perk: Online check-in opens 30 days before sailing
Activity & excursion booking opens 75 days before sailing
Access to all complimentary onboard activities, dining, and entertainment
Automatic enrollment in Castaway Club after your first completed sailing

hiddenPerks.perk: Your serving team will go out of their way to make first-timers feel special — let them know!
First-time cruiser buttons are available at Guest Services — wear one and crew members will give you extra attention
Book your next cruise onboard for a reduced deposit ($250 vs $500+) and $100-$200 onboard credit — you can change dates later

tips.title: Download the Disney Cruise Line app
tips.tip: The Navigator app replaces the paper daily schedule. It shows show times, dining details, deck plans, and lets you message other guests on the ship for free.

tips.title: Bring a lanyard for your Key to the World card
tips.tip: You'll use this card constantly — for your room, charging purchases, and getting on/off the ship. A lanyard keeps it accessible and hard to lose.

tips.title: Set up your onboard account before sailing
tips.tip: Complete online check-in including payment setup and health questionnaire. Upload passport photos. This speeds up your port arrival significantly.

tips.title: Eat lunch at the pool deck immediately after boarding
tips.tip: Cabanas (or the equivalent quick-service spot) is the only dining option open when you first board. Eat first, explore second — you'll have a long day ahead.

tips.title: Attend the Sail-Away Celebration
tips.tip: The deck party as the ship departs is pure magic. Get there early for a good viewing spot. Characters appear, confetti flies, and the excitement of your cruise officially begins.

tips.title: Register kids for youth clubs on Day 1
tips.tip: Lines for youth club registration can be long. Do it early while the ship is still relatively empty and before the Sail-Away party if possible.

levels.label: Silver
levels.description: You've caught the Disney cruise bug. Silver status is earned after your first completed sailing.

benefits.perk: Online check-in opens 33 days before sailing (3 days early)
Activity & excursion booking opens 90 days before sailing (15 days early)
Castaway Club welcome-back gift in your stateroom
Exclusive Castaway Club lanyard on first Silver sailing
Access to Silver-and-above Castaway Club reception onboard
Special 25% off select merchandise discount on embarkation day

hiddenPerks.perk: Silver members sometimes receive complimentary room upgrades when the ship isn't full — book a lower category and get lucky
The extra 15 days for activity booking is a huge advantage for snagging popular spa and dining reservations
Mention your Castaway status to your servers — they often mark special occasions with extra desserts or attention
Silver status counts for everyone in your stateroom — your whole party benefits from your booking windows

tips.title: Request dining changes early
tips.tip: If you want to switch seating times or have a larger table, go to Guest Services on embarkation day. Changes are first-come, first-served and the best options go quickly.

tips.title: Use the laundry rooms strategically
tips.tip: Laundry rooms on Deck 2 and 6 are free to use (detergent costs extra). Go early morning or during dinner when everyone is in the dining rooms. Bring your own detergent pods.

tips.title: Book Palo brunch instead of dinner
tips.tip: Palo brunch on sea days is the same price as dinner but includes both breakfast and lunch items plus the incredible pizza and soufflé. Better value and a more relaxed atmosphere.

tips.title: Get the drink package math right
tips.tip: Calculate how many drinks per day you'd need to break even on a beverage package. For most cruisers, 4-5 alcoholic drinks per day is the breakeven point. Buy individual drinks if you're a light drinker.

tips.title: Check the Navigator for character schedules
tips.tip: Character appearances rotate through specific locations throughout the day. Check your daily Navigator or the app to plan meet & greets and avoid long waits by going to less obvious times.

tips.title: Magnetic hooks are a game-changer
tips.tip: Stateroom walls and doors are metal. Magnetic hooks let you hang hats, lanyards, wet swimsuits, and shoe organizers without damaging anything. Bring at least 4-6.

levels.label: Gold
levels.description: A seasoned cruiser. Gold status unlocks even earlier booking windows and exclusive recognition.

benefits.perk: Online check-in opens 35 days before sailing (5 days early)
Activity & excursion booking opens 105 days before sailing (30 days early)
Gold Castaway Club welcome-back gift (upgraded from Silver)
Exclusive Gold Castaway Club lanyard
Gold-level meet and greet with ship officers
Priority check-in line at the port terminal
Access to all Silver-level benefits

hiddenPerks.perk: Gold members get 105-day booking — that's 30 extra days to grab premium Palo/Remy reservations and the best excursion time slots before most guests
The Gold officer meet & greet is a chance to tour the bridge on some sailings — ask your Guest Services team about availability
Gold and above members occasionally receive Castaway Cay cabana upgrade offers at a discount
At Castaway Cay, Gold members can sometimes access the bike rental and snorkel equipment before general guests

tips.title: Order from tomorrow's menu tonight
tips.tip: Your servers can bring you items from the next evening's menu as extras. If you see something amazing on tomorrow's menu in the Navigator, ask your server to prepare it tonight.

tips.title: Use the room service breakfast trick
tips.tip: Order room service breakfast to arrive 15 minutes before your wake-up time. Fill out the door hanger the night before. It's complimentary and lets you start the day without rushing to a restaurant.

tips.title: Book Castaway Cay cabanas early
tips.tip: Private cabanas on Castaway Cay sell out immediately during the booking window. Set an alarm for the exact opening time. Serenity Bay cabanas are the most peaceful option.

tips.title: Check for onboard booking discounts
tips.tip: Book your next cruise while still on the ship. You'll get a reduced deposit ($250 vs $500+) and an onboard credit ($100-$200). You can change dates and itineraries later.

tips.title: Skip the crowded pool deck on sea days
tips.tip: Head to the Quiet Cove pool or explore the adults-only sundeck areas while families crowd the main pool. The Aqua Mouse/Duck line is shortest in the first 30 minutes after opening.

tips.title: Bring glow sticks and Fish Extender gifts
tips.tip: Fish Extenders are a community gift exchange tradition. Join a Facebook group for your sailing to participate. Also, glow sticks for Pirate Night make the deck party more fun.

levels.label: Platinum
levels.description: Disney Cruise Line royalty. Platinum brings significant booking advantages and exclusive onboard recognition.

benefits.perk: Online check-in opens 38 days before sailing (8 days early)
Activity & excursion booking opens 120 days before sailing (45 days early)
Platinum Castaway Club welcome-back gift (premium tier)
Exclusive Platinum Castaway Club lanyard
Platinum & Pearl exclusive reception with senior ship officers
Priority tendering at ports (board tenders before general guests)
Priority check-in at port terminal
Complimentary specialty dining upgrade on select sailings
All Gold and Silver benefits included

hiddenPerks.perk: Platinum members are first in line for complimentary stateroom upgrades when higher categories go unsold
120-day activity booking means you can book popular time slots months before Silver and new cruisers even have access
Priority tendering is a massive time saver at tender ports — you'll be ashore while others are still waiting
The Platinum/Pearl reception often includes behind-the-scenes tidbits about the ship, upcoming itineraries, and new ships
Some Platinum members report receiving surprise onboard credit or spa discount offers via their stateroom TV
Your Castaway status is noted by the crew — Platinum guests often get preferential seating requests honored more readily

tips.title: Know your Platinum perks
tips.tip: Platinum members get priority tendering, a welcome-aboard gift, earlier check-in and activity windows, and a meet & greet with senior officers. Check the Navigator for the Platinum/Pearl reception.

tips.title: Time your spa visits for port days
tips.tip: The spa runs port day specials and is much quieter when most guests are ashore. Book treatments on port days for the best deals and most relaxing experience.

tips.title: Request specific table assignments
tips.tip: If you've cruised enough to know your preferences, request a specific table size or location during check-in. Window tables and smaller tables for couples are in high demand.

tips.title: Maximize onboard credit stacking
tips.tip: Stack Disney Visa onboard credit, booking onboard credit, and any promotional credits. Gift cards purchased at a discount (Target 5% with RedCard) can be applied to your onboard account.

tips.title: Pack for themed nights
tips.tip: Beyond Pirate Night, some sailings have Marvel, Star Wars, or Frozen themed evenings. Check your itinerary for special theme nights and pack accordingly — costumes and themed outfits add to the fun.

tips.title: Use the app for character wait times
tips.tip: Character meet & greet wait times are displayed in the app. Characters with the shortest waits are often in less obvious locations like the atrium or near restaurants rather than the main character area.

levels.label: Pearl
levels.description: The highest Castaway Club tier. Pearl members are among Disney Cruise Line's most loyal guests, with the best perks available.

benefits.perk: Online check-in opens 40 days before sailing (10 days early)
Activity & excursion booking opens 123 days before sailing (48 days early)
Pearl Castaway Club welcome-back gift (highest tier)
Exclusive Pearl Castaway Club lanyard and pin
Platinum & Pearl exclusive reception with Captain and senior officers
Priority tendering at all ports
Priority check-in with dedicated Pearl line at port
Complimentary specialty dining on select sailings
Exclusive Pearl stateroom gift and welcome letter from the Captain
All Platinum, Gold, and Silver benefits included

hiddenPerks.perk: Pearl members get the absolute earliest booking window at 123 days — practically guaranteed to secure any reservation you want
Some Pearl members report being offered complimentary Concierge-level amenities like fruit plates, chocolate-covered strawberries, or wine on embarkation day
Pearl guests are sometimes personally greeted by name by the Captain or Hotel Director during the voyage
The welcome-back gift has historically included items like exclusive artwork, ornaments, or collectible pins not available for purchase
Pearl members have the best chance of complimentary upgrades — including to Concierge or suite categories when available
On some sailings, Pearl members receive invitations to exclusive behind-the-scenes tours (galley tours, bridge visits, or entertainment backstage)
Your repeat cruiser status is flagged ship-wide — expect crew members to acknowledge your loyalty throughout the voyage

tips.title: The concierge lounge is your secret weapon
tips.tip: If you've upgraded to concierge, the lounge offers complimentary snacks, drinks, and a dedicated concierge team. Even without concierge, Pearl members get select similar perks.

tips.title: Build relationships with your servers
tips.tip: After 25+ cruises, you may start getting the same serving teams. Remember their names, tip generously, and they'll go above and beyond — special desserts, off-menu items, and personalized touches.

tips.title: Combine back-to-back sailings for maximum value
tips.tip: Back-to-back cruises often offer a discount on the second sailing and let you stay onboard during turnaround day. You'll keep your stateroom and enjoy the ship while it's nearly empty.

tips.title: Know the best stateroom locations
tips.tip: Mid-ship on lower decks provides the smoothest ride. Forward staterooms on higher decks offer the best views. Avoid rooms near laundry rooms (noise) or under the pool deck. Check deck plans carefully.

tips.title: Organize group cruises
tips.tip: With your experience, you're the perfect person to organize group sailings. Groups of 8+ staterooms get perks like onboard credit and sometimes a free stateroom for the organizer.

tips.title: Share your knowledge with first-timers
tips.tip: The Disney cruise community thrives on shared knowledge. Help new cruisers at your dinner table, in the lounges, or online. Your decades of experience is incredibly valuable — pay it forward!

levels.label: Concierge
levels.description: The premium stateroom experience. Concierge guests enjoy exclusive spaces, personalized service, and VIP treatment from embarkation to debarkation.

benefits.perk: Private Concierge Lounge with complimentary snacks, beverages, and a dedicated concierge team
Priority check-in with a dedicated Concierge line at the port terminal
Welcome amenity basket in your stateroom with fruit, chocolates, and sparkling wine
Expanded room service menu with premium items not available to other stateroom categories
Exclusive Concierge Sun Deck with private pool, whirlpool, and bar service (select ships)
Priority seating and reservations for specialty dining, spa, and excursions
Complimentary laundry pressing service
Nightly turndown with premium pillow chocolates and bottled water

hiddenPerks.perk: The Concierge Lounge hosts a complimentary happy hour each evening with cocktails, wine, beer, and appetizers — a hidden gem most non-Concierge guests never learn about
Concierge guests sometimes receive invitations for private character meet & greets in the lounge — shorter lines, more one-on-one time, and exclusive photo opportunities
On select sailings, Concierge guests get access to behind-the-scenes events like galley tours, bridge visits, and cast meet & greets not offered to other guests
Your Concierge team can often arrange special celebrations — custom cakes, in-room decorations, or surprise character visits for birthdays and anniversaries
Concierge staterooms on some ships have oversized verandahs with premium furniture, offering significantly more outdoor space than standard Verandah rooms

tips.title: Make friends with your Concierge team on Day 1
tips.tip: Visit the Concierge Lounge immediately after boarding and introduce yourself to the team. They can make dining changes, book excursions, and handle special requests faster than Guest Services. Building rapport early means better service all cruise.

tips.title: Don't skip the happy hour
tips.tip: The Concierge Lounge happy hour (typically 5-7 PM) offers complimentary cocktails and appetizers that rival specialty dining. It's the best-kept secret of the Concierge experience and saves you money on bar purchases throughout the trip.

tips.title: Use the expanded room service menu
tips.tip: Concierge guests get access to premium room service items including shrimp cocktail, filet mignon, and lobster tail. Order breakfast to your stateroom for a luxurious morning on your private verandah.

tips.title: The Sun Deck is worth early mornings
tips.tip: The Concierge Sun Deck is rarely crowded before 10 AM. Grab a lounge chair, order a coffee from the bar, and enjoy the private pool and ocean views without the main deck crowds.

tips.title: Ask about Castaway Cay cabana priority
tips.tip: Concierge guests often get first access to Castaway Cay cabana reservations. Ask your Concierge team about availability before your island day — they can sometimes secure cabanas that appear sold out.

tips.title: Is Concierge worth the upgrade?
tips.tip: For special occasions, absolutely. For families, the extra space and Sun Deck are game-changers. For frequent cruisers who already have Pearl status, the overlap in perks is significant — evaluate whether the premium price adds enough beyond your existing Castaway Club benefits.
